page.title: Marbella Yacht Charter Weather in April: Conditions, Tips & Best Times

data.meta_description: Plan your Marbella yacht charter in April with confidence. Find March–May sea temps, wind stats, rainfall averages, and insider tips for the best day to sail.
data.summary: April in Marbella offers mild air (15–21°C) and calm sea conditions, making it a reliable shoulder month for yacht charters. This guide covers March–May weather data, wind patterns, rainfall averages, and insider tips to choose the best day for your Puerto Banús departure.
key_facts: Air temperature in April: 15°C low / 21°C high.
Sea temperature in April: 16–17°C (warms to 18°C by May).
Prevailing wind: light onshore breeze, 10–15 knots.
Sunshine: 8 hours per day (UV index moderate).
Water clarity: good outside Marbella Bay after rain clears.
Best time to sail: 11:00–16:00 when sea breeze peaks.
All our charters include skipper, fuel, drinks & snacks – no hidden fees.

faq.q: Is April a good month for a yacht charter in Marbella?
faq.a: Yes. April offers mild weather, fewer crowds than summer, and lower charter rates. Sea conditions are generally calm, with light winds and manageable swell. You’ll enjoy a relaxed atmosphere on the water, though swimming may feel brisk at 16–17°C.

faq.q: What is the sea temperature in Marbella in April?
faq.a: Sea temperature in April averages 16–17°C. It warms to 18°C by early May. If you plan to swim or snorkel, a shorty wetsuit is recommended for comfort. For sunbathing and cruising, the temperature is pleasant.

faq.q: How windy is Marbella in April?
faq.a: April typically sees light onshore breezes of 10–15 knots, with occasional gusts up to 20 knots during frontal passages. The afternoon sea breeze (poniente) is common, making sailing conditions favourable. Check the forecast 48 hours ahead for any Levante (easterly) events.

faq.q: Does it rain a lot in Marbella in April?
faq.a: April averages 35mm of rain over about 6 days, mostly as short showers or thunderstorms. Rain usually clears quickly, leaving bright skies. A morning shower is possible, but afternoons are generally dry and sunny.

faq.q: What should I wear on a yacht charter in April?
faq.a: Layers are key: a light sweater or fleece for early mornings/evenings, a waterproof jacket just in case, and shorts or a swimsuit for midday sun. Sunscreen (SPF 30+) and sunglasses are essential. Deck shoes with non-slip soles are recommended.

faq.q: What is the best time of day for a charter in April?
faq.a: The best window is 11:00 to 16:00, when air temperatures peak at 21°C and the sea breeze is strongest. Mornings can be cooler (15°C), so a later start maximises comfort. Sunset cruises are also pleasant but bring a jacket.

faq.q: Which boats are best for April weather?
faq.a: Any of our fleet suits April conditions. For a group of 9–12, the Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) offers sheltered flybridge seating. The Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) has spacious decks for sunbathing. For smaller groups, the Speedboat (7m, 6 pax) is agile in light winds.

faq.q: Can I swim or snorkel in April?
faq.a: Yes, but the water is cool (16–17°C). Snorkelling is possible with a wetsuit. Popular spots like Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés have good visibility after settled weather. If swimming isn't your priority, the scenic coastline from Puerto Banús to Estepona is still beautiful for cruising.

data.body_html:
April is a sweet spot for a Marbella yacht charter. The winter chill has passed, summer crowds haven't arrived, and the sea is calm enough for comfortable cruising. Air temperatures hover between 15°C and 21°C, sea water sits at 16–17°C, and you get around 8 hours of sunshine daily. This guide breaks down the numbers, wind patterns, rainfall odds, and practical tips so you can pick the best day to sail from Puerto Banús.

April Air & Sea Temperatures

April in Marbella is mild and pleasant. Average highs reach 21°C in the afternoon, dropping to 15°C overnight. Sea temperature lags behind at 16–17°C, warming to 18°C by early May. For comparison, March averages 12–18°C air and 15°C sea, while May jumps to 16–24°C air and 18°C sea. If you plan to swim, bring a shorty wetsuit – the water is brisk but refreshing after a sunny day.

Wind & Sea Conditions

The prevailing wind in April is the poniente (westerly), a light onshore breeze of 10–15 knots. Occasional Levante (easterly) events can push 20–25 knots, but these are brief. Swell height averages 0.5–1m, ideal for motor yachts and catamarans. The afternoon sea breeze kicks in around 11:00, making the 11:00–16:00 window the smoothest for cruising. Early mornings are glassy but cooler.

Rainfall & Sunshine

April sees roughly 35mm of rain over 6 days – mostly short showers or afternoon thunderstorms. Rain clears quickly, leaving bright skies. Sunshine averages 8 hours per day, with UV index at 5 (moderate). Sunscreen is essential even on overcast days. For comparison, March gets 50mm over 7 rainy days, while May drops to 25mm over 4 days.

Best Times for a Charter

The sweet spot is 11:00–16:00, when air temperatures peak and the sea breeze is strongest. Morning charters (10:00 start) are cooler but offer calm seas. Sunset cruises (17:00–19:00) are popular but require a jacket as temperatures drop to 15°C. For the best balance of warmth and conditions, book a 4-hour charter departing at 11:00. Our Marbella yacht charter fleet includes options for every group size.

Which Boats Suit April Weather?

All our boats handle April conditions well. For a group of 9–12, the Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) offers a sheltered flybridge with panoramic views. The Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) has spacious decks for sunbathing. For smaller groups, the Speedboat (7m, 6 pax) is agile in light winds. Catamarans like the Lagoon 380 (11.6m, 10 pax) provide stable cruising and ample shade.

What to Pack for an April Charter

  • Light sweater or fleece for mornings/evenings
  • Waterproof jacket (just in case of a shower)
  • Shorts, swimsuit, and a towel
  • Sunscreen (SPF 30+) and sunglasses
  • Deck shoes or non-slip sandals
  • Shorty wetsuit if you plan to swim

Popular April Destinations

From Puerto Banús, head west towards Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés for sheltered coves. East to Cabopino offers a quiet beach and the Artola dunes. For a longer cruise, Estepona’s marina is 12 NM away (about 1 hour). The water clarity is best after a few settled days – check the forecast before booking.

Frequently asked questions

Is April a good month for a yacht charter in Marbella?

Yes. April offers mild weather, fewer crowds than summer, and lower charter rates. Sea conditions are generally calm, with light winds and manageable swell. You’ll enjoy a relaxed atmosphere on the water, though swimming may feel brisk at 16–17°C.

What is the sea temperature in Marbella in April?

Sea temperature in April averages 16–17°C. It warms to 18°C by early May. If you plan to swim or snorkel, a shorty wetsuit is recommended for comfort. For sunbathing and cruising, the temperature is pleasant.

How windy is Marbella in April?

April typically sees light onshore breezes of 10–15 knots, with occasional gusts up to 20 knots during frontal passages. The afternoon sea breeze (poniente) is common, making sailing conditions favourable. Check the forecast 48 hours ahead for any Levante (easterly) events.

Does it rain a lot in Marbella in April?

April averages 35mm of rain over about 6 days, mostly as short showers or thunderstorms. Rain usually clears quickly, leaving bright skies. A morning shower is possible, but afternoons are generally dry and sunny.

What should I wear on a yacht charter in April?

Layers are key: a light sweater or fleece for early mornings/evenings, a waterproof jacket just in case, and shorts or a swimsuit for midday sun. Sunscreen (SPF 30+) and sunglasses are essential. Deck shoes with non-slip soles are recommended.

What is the best time of day for a charter in April?

The best window is 11:00 to 16:00, when air temperatures peak at 21°C and the sea breeze is strongest. Mornings can be cooler (15°C), so a later start maximises comfort. Sunset cruises are also pleasant but bring a jacket.

Which boats are best for April weather?

Any of our fleet suits April conditions. For a group of 9–12, the Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) offers sheltered flybridge seating. The Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) has spacious decks for sunbathing. For smaller groups, the Speedboat (7m, 6 pax) is agile in light winds.

Can I swim or snorkel in April?

Yes, but the water is cool (16–17°C). Snorkelling is possible with a wetsuit. Popular spots like Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés have good visibility after settled weather. If swimming isn't your priority, the scenic coastline from Puerto Banús to Estepona is still beautiful for cruising.
